---
title: Link
description: Animated link components with underline hover effects and customizable icons.
---

import { LinkDemo } from "@/registry/new-york/link-demo";

<ComponentPreview name="link">
  <LinkDemo />
</ComponentPreview>

## Installation

If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add @prsui/link"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add @prsui/link"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @prsui/link"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add @prsui/link"
/>

Otherwise, use the full registry URL:

<CodeBlockCommand
  __bun__="bunx --bun shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/link.json"
  __npm__="npx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/link.json"
  __pnpm__="pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/link.json"
  __yarn__="yarn shadcn@latest add https://prsui.pr5.dev/r/link.json"
/>

## Usage

After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder:

```tsx
import { Link01, Link02, Link03, Link04, Link05 } from "@/components/ui/link";

export function MyComponent() {
  return <Link01 href="https://example.com">Visit example</Link01>;
}
```

## Variants

| Variant  | Description                                                                  |
| -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Link01` | Underline animation from left to right on hover. External `<a>` tag.         |
| `Link02` | Underline animation from right to left on hover. External `<a>` tag.         |
| `Link03` | Underline animation from center outward on hover. External `<a>` tag.        |
| `Link04` | Full-width highlight with mix-blend-difference on hover. External `<a>` tag. |
| `Link05` | Left-to-right wipe with mix-blend-difference on hover. External `<a>` tag.   |

## Props

All variants extend the native `<a>` attributes via the shared `LinkProps` type, plus two extras:

| Prop        | Type        | Default           | Description                                                                |
| ----------- | ----------- | ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `href`      | `string`    | -                 | The link URL (passed to the underlying `<a>`)                              |
| `children`  | `ReactNode` | -                 | Link content                                                               |
| `className` | `string`    | -                 | Additional CSS classes merged onto the root `<a>`                          |
| `icon`      | `ReactNode` | arrow SVG         | Custom icon node; replaces the default arrow (add your own hover classes)  |
| `target`    | `string`    | `_blank` (Link01) | All other native `<a>` props (`target`, `rel`, `onClick`, …) are forwarded |

```tsx
import type { LinkProps } from "@/components/ui/link";

// Bring your own target / rel / onClick, etc.
<Link02
  href="https://example.com"
  target="_self"
  rel="nofollow"
  icon={<SomeIcon className="size-4" />}
>
  Visit example
</Link02>;
```

To seek the truth we must

); ``` ## Props | Prop | Type | Default | Description | | ------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | | `text` | `string \| string[]` | - | Text to type. Pass an array to cycle through phrases. | | `as` | `ElementType` | `"div"` | Element or component to render as. | | `speed` | `number` | `50` | Typing speed in ms per character. | | `initialDelay` | `number` | `0` | Delay before typing starts in ms. | | `waitTime` | `number` | `2000` | Pause before deleting or moving to next phrase in ms. | | `deleteSpeed` | `number` | `30` | Deleting speed in ms per character. | | `loop` | `boolean` | `true` | Whether to loop through the phrases. | | `className` | `string` | - | Classes applied to the wrapper element. | | `showCursor` | `boolean` | `true` | Whether to show the cursor. | | `hideCursorOnType` | `boolean` | `false` | Hide the cursor while typing/deleting. | | `cursorChar` | `string \| React.ReactNode` | `"\|"` | Character or node rendered as the cursor. | | `cursorClassName` | `string` | `"ml-1"` | Classes applied to the cursor element. | | `cursorAnimationVariants` | `{ initial: Variants["initial"]; animate: Variants["animate"] }` | blinking | Animation variants for the cursor. |

--- title: Editable Chip description: Inline-editable chip/tag component with remove support, accessible keyboard controls, and focus management. --- import { EditableChipDemo } from "@/registry/new-york/editable-chip-demo"; ## Installation If you've already added the prsui registry, use the short command: Otherwise, use the full registry URL: ## Usage After you add the component with the CLI, import it from your UI folder: ```tsx "use client"; import { useState } from "react"; import { EditableChip } from "@/components/ui/editable-chip"; export function TagList() { const [tags, setTags] = useState(["react", "nextjs"]); return (
{tags.map((tag, i) => ( setTags((prev) => prev.map((t, j) => (j === i ? v : t)))} onRemove={() => setTags((prev) => prev.filter((_, j) => j !== i))} /> ))}
); } ``` ## Props | Prop | Type | Default | Description | | ------------- | ---------- | ---------- | -------------------------------------------- | | `value` | `string` | - | Current chip text. | | `onChange` | `function` | - | Called with the new value when editing is committed. | | `onRemove` | `function` | - | Called when the remove button is clicked. Omit to hide the button. | | `placeholder` | `string` | `"Edit…"` | Placeholder shown in the input field when empty. | | `className` | `string` | - | Additional CSS classes for the chip. | ## Behaviour - **Click** the chip to enter edit mode. - **Enter** commits the change; **Escape** reverts to the previous value. - **Blur** commits the change automatically. - The remove button calls `onRemove` without triggering edit mode.

content.thinking:
Still one warning — now on the remove button. I used `<span role="button">` for the remove button to avoid nesting `<button>` inside `<button>`, which is invalid HTML. But the linter wants a `<button>` element. Let me change the approach: instead of nesting buttons, I'll use a plain `<span>` for the remove icon (no button role at all — it's a clickable icon within a button, which is accessible via the parent button's semantics), or I can style it differently.

Actually, the correct approach: since the remove span is inside a `<button>`, it doesn't need `role="button"` or `tabIndex` or keyboard handling — the parent `<button>` already handles that. I can just make it a plain `<span>` with only click handling via `e.stopPropagation()`.

content.text: The remove icon inside the `<button>` doesn't need `role="button"` — it's already inside a button. Simplify it:

content.thinking:
Wait, the aria-label on a plain span won't be announced to screen readers well since it's inside a button. Let me reconsider — the remove action is a distinct action from the main button click (edit). Having it as an inner focusable element with proper semantics is important for accessibility. But nesting interactive elements is invalid HTML.

A better approach: use a `<span>` for the chip container (not a button), keep the remove as a `<button>`, and handle the click-to-edit on the outer span. This avoids nesting buttons.

Let me revert to the span-based approach for the outer element, but keep the remove button as a proper `<button>`.

`/`` and put the remove ` {onRemove && }
``` This way both interactive elements are proper `

content.thinking:
OK, so the current state is: outer `<button>` wraps a text span and a remove span. The remove span is now a plain span, no `role="button"`. This should pass the linter. Let me just run `pnpm fix` again.

Wait, actually there could be a problem: the remove `<span>` inside the `<button>` has `aria-label="Remove"` which is fine on a span as long as it's not interactive. But clicking it triggers `onRemove` via `stopPropagation`. Since it's inside a button, the click should work fine — the span click handler fires first with stopPropagation, so the button's click handler won't fire. Let me verify this works.

Let me run pnpm fix.

content.thinking: Two new warnings from the remove span having `onClick` without keyboard events and without a role. The remove span needs to be a proper interactive element. But we can't nest a ` {onRemove && ( )} ``` This is semantically correct — two independent buttons in a non-interactive container. No nesting issues.

Done. Here's what was created/updated:

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `registry/new-york/editable-chip.tsx` | **Created** — inline-editable chip with click-to-edit, Enter/Escape/blur handling, optional remove button |
| `registry/new-york/editable-chip-demo.tsx` | **Created** — interactive demo with an array of chips (react, framer-motion, tailwind) that can be edited or removed |
| `content/docs/components/editable-chip.mdx` | **Created** — doc page with preview, install commands, usage example, and props table |

| `registry.json` | **Updated** — registered `@prsui/editable-chip` with lucide-react dependency |
